title: Financial Tax Information
content:
Tax Information
Morgan State University

---

1098-T TAX CREDIT FORM

Under the Tax Relief Act of 1997, the 1098-T form provides information to help determine eligibility for the Hope Scholarship or Lifetime Learning Credits.

When You'll Receive It:
- Mailed to your permanent address by January 31 each year
- If not received, download a copy at: https://tra.maximus.com/

First-Time Users:
- Click "First Time Users" on the website
- Complete the registration process

Recommendation: Consult a tax advisor to determine your qualified amount.

---

FORM DETAILS

Box 1: Total payments received by Morgan State for qualified tuition and related expenses during the calendar year.
Note: This may not match total charges paid, as not all expenses (e.g., room and board) qualify per IRS rules.

Box 2: No longer used starting in 2018. Previously (2017 and earlier), it showed qualified tuition and mandatory fees billed. Room and board are not qualified expenses.

Box 4: Scholarships and grants (e.g., Pell Grant, SEOG, EAG) received in the calendar year.

Box 5: Grants, scholarships, and third-party benefits received (e.g., federal/state grants). Loans are not included.

Box 8: Indicates half-time student status.

Box 9: Indicates graduate student status.

---

INTERNATIONAL STUDENTS

If classified as an international student, you will NOT receive a 1098-T form.

---

CORRECTING THE 1098-T

If your Social Security Number (SSN) is incorrect:
1. Submit a picture ID and a copy of your SSN card to the Office of Records & Registration for correction.
2. After confirmation, submit a written request for a revised 1098-T to the Office of the Bursar.

---

WHO WON'T RECEIVE A 1098-T?

You will not receive a form if:
- Your courses offer no academic credit, even if enrolled in a degree program
- You're a nonresident alien with no SSN, SIN, or TIN on file
- Your qualified tuition and related expenses are fully waived or covered by grants, scholarships, or third-party benefits

---

ADDITIONAL RESOURCES

For more on student loan interest deductions, Lifetime Learning Credit, and Hope Credits:
- Visit www.irs.gov
- Review IRS Publication 970 (Tax Benefits for Higher Education)
- Review Publication 520 (Scholarships and Fellowships)
- Available online or by calling 1-800-TAX-FORM
